mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 8.5 percent, it signals a structural labor market recovery that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns following UNRATE crossings below 8.5% are statistically indistinguishable from zero or negative across three consecutive post-2000 cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts cyclical equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of co-occurring HOUST and RSAFS uptick instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production accelerating above a 1.0 percent month-over-month growth rate indicates capacity utilization normalization and supply chain healing, which directly supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat in over 60% of INDPRO growth spikes exceeding 1.0% during the 2009-2015 sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly real GDP growth directly tracks corporate revenue trends, creating a persistent positive correlation that drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days without requiring regime filters.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between quarterly GDPC1 changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month compresses real yields and triggers market expectations for aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns are positive in more than 50% of CPIAUCSL monthly increases exceeding 0.3% across the 2000-2015 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market healing, which reduces wage-price spiral fears and supports sustained equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decline signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of occurrences during the 2010-2015 window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion environment, real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent annualized historically precedes margin compression and monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where annualized GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0% during late-cycle phases.
